title: BTQ Technologies and TIDAL PWR Partner to Develop Trusted Quantum Data Center Reference Architectures - Quantum Computing Report
gist: BTQ Technologies (Nasdaq: BTQ) and Texas power/data-center developer TIDAL PWR are partnering to create standardized reference architectures for integrating QPUs and post-quantum cryptography into AI/HPC data centers, targeting the procurement and crypto-agility layer as quantum and AI workloads converge in shared facilities.

insights.claim: BTQ Technologies and TIDAL PWR are building a standardized, repeatable blueprint for integrating QPUs and post-quantum security protocols directly into next-generation AI and HPC data center infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A reference-architecture play positions BTQ at the integration standard layer, potentially a recurring advisory and IP revenue channel.
insights.quote: building a standardized, repeatable blueprint for integrating quantum processing units (QPUs) and post-quantum security protocols directly into next-generation AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) data center infrastructure
insights.locator: The partnership focuses on building a standardized

insights.claim: The initiative targets the operational layer between raw quantum research and commercial real estate buildouts, so operators can integrate quantum systems without treating them as open-ended research projects.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Framing quantum as a procurable, standardized deployment rather than an R&D project is what unlocks enterprise adoption at scale.
insights.quote: the joint initiative targets the operational layer that sits between raw quantum research and commercial real estate buildouts
insights.locator: Rather than focusing solely on hardware execution

insights.claim: BTQ will lead the solutions architecture, providing operators a technical framework for how to procure, secure, and deploy quantum hardware alongside classical compute stacks.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: BTQ owning the architecture role makes it a gatekeeper for vendor selection into these facilities.
insights.locator: BTQ will lead the solutions architecture

themes.theme: Post-Quantum Crypto-Agility and Supply Chain Trust

insights.claim: The partnership defines crypto-agile architectural standards integrating post-quantum cryptography to protect AI training models, inference traffic, and enterprise data from future quantum decryption risks.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Linking PQC directly to protecting AI model IP creates a concrete enterprise demand driver for post-quantum security ahead of cryptographically relevant quantum computers.
insights.quote: integrate post-quantum cryptography (PQC) to protect sensitive AI training models, inference traffic, and enterprise data pathways from future quantum decryption risks

insights.claim: A defined workstream establishes trusted vendor assessment guidelines for chips, devices, and physical hardware to guard against quantum supply chain vulnerabilities.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Trusted-vendor criteria could become a de facto qualification gate that advantages compliant hardware suppliers.

insights.claim: BTQ is pursuing a full-stack neutral-atom quantum computing development track, referenced in the facility integration roadmaps workstream.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Reveals BTQ has its own neutral-atom hardware ambition, not just a security-integrator identity, a broader bet to watch.
insights.quote: including BTQ’s full-stack neutral-atom quantum computing development track

themes.theme: Power as the Binding Datacenter Constraint

insights.claim: Power availability remains one of the primary growth constraints for large-scale AI data center developments, which is why the partnership pairs quantum architecture with TIDAL PWR's power generation and site development.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Reinforces power as the gating factor for AI/quantum datacenter growth, elevating power-plus-site developers as strategic partners.
insights.quote: Power availability remains one of the primary growth constraints for large-scale AI data center developments
insights.locator: Bridging Power Infrastructure and Advanced Computing Requirements

insights.claim: The companies plan a repeatable deployment model aimed at hyperscalers, managed service providers, and enterprise facility operators, starting with joint architecture and technical planning before commercial buildouts.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Early-stage planning only means execution risk is high; watch for movement from framework to actual facility deployments.
insights.quote: The initial phase will focus on joint architecture and technical planning, establishing a foundational reference framework before expanding into deeper commercial and facility-specific deployment projects.
insights.locator: The initial phase will focus on joint architecture
